data.title: SK Hynix chief says AI not in bubble, but stocks might face correction: report
data.description:
Chey Tae-won, chair of SK Group, the conglomerate that owns the prolific memory maker SK Hynix (OTCPK:HXSC.F), said during a forum in Seoul that the artificial intelligence industry is not in a bubble, according to Reuters.

However, investor exuberance has led to sky-high valuations, which might face some corrections.

"But when you look at the stock markets, they rose too fast and too much, and I think it is natural that there could be some period of corrections," he said, according to the report.

SK Hynix provides memory chips to Nvidia (NVDA), the Magnificent Seven member that has benefited greatly from the rise of AI due to its powerful graphics processing units. In October, the South Korea-based company reported record-high revenue and has already sold out its memory chip production for the year ahead due to the rise of AI.

Multiple memory companies have experienced especially strong gains during 2025. Micron Technology (MU) shares have surged 175% year to date, while Seagate Technology (STX) has rocketed more than 200%. 

Broadcom (AVGO) has increased nearly 70% year to date, and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares have climbed 81%. Nvidia shares have advanced 36%. Intel (INTC) shares have more than doubled in 2025.

Some software companies have also enjoyed the AI wave. Palantir (PLTR) has shot up 135%, while AppLovin (APP) has risen 116%.

data.title: India weighs phone-location surveillance; Apple, Google, and Samsung protest: Reuters
data.description:
The Indian government is reviewing a telecom industry proposal to force smartphone firms to enable satellite location tracking that is always activated, Reuters reported on Friday, citing documents, emails, and sources.

The latest move by the Indian government is being opposed by Apple (AAPL), Google (GOOGL), and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) due to privacy concerns, the report added. 

The reported move comes after a privacy debate erupted in India this week after the government scrapped an order to preload a state-run cybersecurity app on all new devices after politicians, privacy advocates, and global tech companies raised concerns over surveillance fears. 

The Cellular Operators Association of India (COAI), which represents Reliance's Jio and Bharti Airtel, has proposed that precise user locations should only be provided if the government orders smartphone makers to activate A-GPS technology, according to the Reuters report. 

That would require location services to always be activated in smartphones with no option for users to disable them. 

Apple (AAPL), Google (GOOGL), and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) have told New Delhi that it should not be mandated, the report said. 

At this point, no policy decision has been made by the IT or home ministries.

data.title: Kroger to pay Ocado $350M for warehouse closures
data.description:
Kroger (KR) will pay online grocer and automation platform provider Ocado $350M in cash following the grocer’s decision to close three Ocado-automated warehouses and adjust its e-commerce strategy.

The payout marks a significant shift from the companies' original 2018 partnership. To capitalize on the growth of e-commerce and improve its fulfillment capabilities, Kroger (KR) partnered with UK-based Ocado to build 20 state-of-the-art, automated customer fulfillment centers ((CFC)) across the U.S.

But after opening just eight facilities, Kroger (KR) conceded that the substantial investment failed to pay off as the Ocado-tooled facilities struggled with their inconvenient locations, lack of volume, and failure to deliver the speed that U.S. consumers were accustomed to from Amazon (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT).

Additionally, with competitors increasing their scale and speed, Kroger’s (KR) fulfillment centers were unable to compete effectively to justify the investment.

In 2023, Kroger (KR) suspended further development of Ocado-tooled CFCs as the existing facilities “did not meet the benchmarks set for success.” By September, the grocer made the decision to shutter the three existing facilities, taking a $2.6B impairment charge to shift to its network of stores to complete online orders, expanding its relationships with Instacart (CART), DoorDash (DASH), and Uber Eats (UBER).

Ocado said it will continue to work with Kroger (KR) on the five existing sites, with another expected to open in 2026 in Phoenix, Arizona. The Charlotte, North Carolina CFC has been cancelled.

Shares of Ocado Group were as much as 10% higher on the London exchange, while Kroger (KR) opened fractionally lower on Friday, marking its third day in the red.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: HPE, RBRK, SOFI, COO, NFLX, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Friday as investors positioned ahead of the delayed September PCE inflation report, a pivotal indicator for the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision on December 10.​

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Rubrik (RBRK) +20% - Shares jumped after the company posted a strong Q3, with revenue up 48% Y/Y and subscription revenue up 52% Y/Y. Free cash flow came in at $76.9M, sharply higher than $15.6M a year ago. For Q4, the company expects revenue of $341M–$343M, ahead of the ~$325M estimate. Rubrik also raised its FY2026 outlook, guiding revenue to $1.28B (vs. prior $1.23B and in line with estimates) and boosting its free cash flow forecast to $194M–$202M from $145M–$155M.
  • Cooper Cos. (COO) +13% – Shares jumped after the company launched a comprehensive strategic review and appointed Colleen Jay as its new board chair. The review will assess its business portfolio, corporate structure, operations, and capital allocation priorities, with potential outcomes including partnerships, joint ventures, divestitures, mergers, or other transactions. The move follows reports that activist investor Jana Partners took a stake in October and is pushing for strategic alternatives, including a potential combination of Cooper’s contact lens unit with Bausch + Lomb (BLCO). While the review is underway, Cooper plans to continue repurchasing shares under its recently announced $2B buyback authorisation.
  • ChargePoint (CHPT) +8% – Shares rallied after the EV-charging company delivered stronger-than-expected results, with revenue up 6% Y/Y to $106M, topping the high end of its guidance range. Subscription revenue rose 15% to $42M, while GAAP gross margin improved to 31% and non-GAAP margin held at a record 33%. The company also highlighted a post-quarter debt reduction of $172M, cutting its total by more than half. For FQ4, ChargePoint guided revenue to $100M–$110M, with the $105M midpoint coming in ahead of consensus expectations of $102.89M.
  • Ulta Beauty (ULTA) +6% – Shares rose after the retailer delivered a standout Q3, posting strong beats on both revenue and earnings along with comp-store growth that nearly doubled expectations. Reflecting the robust quarter and confidence in a strong holiday season, the company raised its full-year outlook. Ulta now expects sales of roughly $12.3B, above prior guidance and ahead of the consensus of $12.06B, with comps now projected to rise 4.4%–4.7%. EPS guidance was lifted to $25.20–$25.50 from the prior $23.85 and $24.30, topping estimates of $24.57, and operating margin expectations were also raised to 12.3%–12.4% from 11.9%–12.0%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Parsons (PSN) -22% – Shares tumbled after the Federal Aviation Administration awarded a multibillion-dollar contract to modernise the U.S. air traffic control system to Peraton, rather than Parsons. The FAA has $12.5B in initial funding for the overhaul, though the total project cost is estimated at ~$31.5B. Analysts flagged the decision as a major setback. Raymond James downgraded the PSN to Market Perform from Strong Buy, saying the outcome “will be a major surprise to investors” and removes meaningful upside potential for 2026–27. William Blair also trimmed estimates, lowering its FY2026 revenue forecast to $6.51B from $6.71B and adjusted EBITDA to $631M from $643M.
  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) -9% - Shares dropped sharply after the company delivered mixed fiscal Q4 results and issued guidance that fell short of several Street expectations. For FQ1, HPE projected adjusted EPS of $0.57–$0.61, topping the $0.53 consensus, but guided revenue to $9B–$9.4B, well under the anticipated $9.87B. Full-year adjusted EPS is now expected to come in at $2.25–$2.45, with the midpoint just below estimates, while free cash flow of $1.7B–$2B landed meaningfully under the $2.2B forecast.
  • SentinelOne (S) -9% – Despite the Q3 beat, shares fell as the company issued a softer-than-expected Q4 revenue outlook, guiding to $271M vs. the Street’s $271.89M estimate. FY2025 revenue is projected at $1.001B, broadly in line with expectations. The update came alongside news that CFO Barbara Larson will step down in mid-January 2026, with Chief Growth Officer Barry Padgett set to assume the interim CFO role.
  • SoFi Technologies (SOFI) -7% – Shares slipped after the company priced its public offering of 54.5M shares at $27.50, raising an expected ~$1.5B. Underwriters have a 30-day option to buy an additional ~8.2M shares. The deal is slated to close on December 8, 2025, with proceeds earmarked for general corporate purposes, strengthening capital, and supporting future growth initiatives.
  • DocuSign (DOCU) -7% - Shares slid even though the company posted Q3 results and guidance that topped expectations. For Q4, it expects revenue of $825M–$829M, with the $827M midpoint just shy of the ~$827.4M consensus. Subscription revenue is projected at $808M–$812M, and billings at $992M–$1B. Adjusted gross margin is seen at 80.8%–81.1%. The company also raised its full-year outlook, now guiding revenue to $3.208B–$3.212B, above its prior $3.19B–$3.2B range. Subscription revenue is expected at $3.14B–$3.144B, with FY billings forecast at $3.38B–$3.39B. Adjusted gross margin is now seen at 81.7%–81.8%, and adjusted operating margin at 29.8%–29.9%.
  • Netflix (NFLX) -4% - Shares slipped after the company announced it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), including its film and TV studios and HBO/HBO Max, for an enterprise value of ~$82.7B and an implied equity value of ~$72B. Under the terms of the agreement, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash and $4.501 in shares of Netflix common stock for each share of WBD common stock. The deal ends a weeks-long bidding battle in which Netflix’s nearly $28-per-share offer topped Paramount–Skydance’s bid of roughly $24. The acquisition gives Netflix control of marquee franchises such as Game of Thrones, DC Comics, and Harry Potter, marking a major shift in Hollywood as the streaming giant makes its largest-ever content move. Co-CEO Ted Sarandos said the combined companies will “help define the next century of storytelling.” WBD shares rose over 4%, while Paramount (PSKY) slipped more than 2%.

data.title: Stock market rises ahead of inflation gauge; Netflix to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery
data.description:
Wall Street was higher on Friday, as traders looked forward to fresh inflation data in hopes of gauging the Federal Reserve's monetary policy. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.3%. 

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.11%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.55%.

"Markets saw a modest risk-on move yesterday, as a decent batch of US data saw investors price in fewer rate cuts next year," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

Netflix (NFLX) said it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO, for a total enterprise value of ~$82.7 billion.

The personal income and outlays will land during market hours. Income and spending are both expected to be up a robust 0.4% on the month. PCE prices are seen up 0.3% on the month for total and core. 

The consumer sentiment report will also be released at the same time. 52.0 is expected for the first read in December, versus an abysmal 51.0 in the November final.

data.title: Marvell rises as Citi resumes with Buy rating
data.description:
• Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rose 1% on Friday after investment firm Citi resumed coverage on the semiconductor company with a Buy rating.
  • “We believe Marvell will see accelerating Y/Y sales growth in 2026/27 driven by AI data centers,” Citi analyst Atif Malik wrote in a note to clients. “Marvell guided FY27/28 sales to grow ~20%/30% driven by multiple data center AI demand drivers. Moreover, the company has announced the acquisition of Celestial AI. We lift our FY26/27/28 EPS to $2.84/$3.56/$4.99 on higher data center sales outlook.” 
  • Malik also put a $114 price target on Marvell shares.

data.title: Virtus Investment Partners to acquire majority stake in Keystone National
data.description:
Virtus Investment Partners (VRTS) announced on Friday the acquisition of a majority interest in investment manager Keystone National Group for $200M at deal closing.

The transaction consideration also includes up to a $170M of deferred consideration, including earnout payments subject to the achievement of future revenue targets.

The acquisition is expected to be funded using existing balance sheet resources.

Keystone specializes in asset-centric private credit and also manages two private REITs specializing in real estate bridge lending and real estate equity.

The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Unity Software jumps on back of Wells Fargo upgrade
data.description:
• Unity Software (U) shares jumped 4.5% in premarket trading on Friday after Wells Fargo upgraded the video game software company. 
  • “We upgrade U to [Overweight from Equal-Weight] on 1) favorable industry backdrop in 2026 and 2) direct exposure to the direct payments migration w/'Unity Commerce,'” analyst Alec Brondolo wrote in a note to clients. “We raise FY26 grow segment rev growth to +17% y/y from +14% prior on faster industry growth. Unity announced 'Unity Commerce' 10/22 to facilitate the transition from app store to direct payments. We see U as well positioned vs. incumbents Xsolla, Appcharge & Aghanim. Now modeling $55M 2027 Unity Commerce revs, 8% upside [versus] our prior Create segment [revenues].”
  • Brondolo also raised his price target on Unity to $51 from $42.

data.title: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son plans to develop Trump Industrial Parks
data.description:
The Japanese billionaire tech investor Masayoshi Son, CEO of SoftBank, is reportedly mustering billions of dollars to build Trump-branded industrial parks in the United States after months of discussions with officials at the White House and Commerce Department.

The parks would be largely built on federal land, with funds pledged by the Japanese government as part of the recent trade deal, the Wall Street Journal reported.

The facilities would produce components for artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure.

President Donald Trump signalled that he supports the Son’s plan, the reported stated, according to people familiar with the matter.

SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF) and the Trump administration are continuing the discussion.

According to people who attended the meetings, Son pitched Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and other U.S. officials on his plans to build a $1 trillion industrial city in the Arizona desert, north of Phoenix.

Over the summer, the SoftBank CEO pitched the project to Trump directly at a meeting, people familiar with the matter said.

However, the blueprint for the project changed after several meetings, according to an administration official, as the administration began pushing for a network of ‘Trump Industrial Parks' with manufacturing infrastructure built on federal land, instead of one mega development.

As per the current plans, softBank will help construct factory clusters that produce fiber-optic cables, data-center equipment and finally AI chips.

Japanese tech companies are expected to supply much of the expertise behind these new ventures, while the federal government will own the parks once built.  

An administration official reportedly stated that the initial funding for these projects could be released as early as the first quarter of 2026.

White House spokesperson Kush Desai reportedly said in a statement that President Trump’s relationship with business leaders across industries and geographies are playing a key role in securing trillions in investments that are helping build America’s next Golden Age.

As  part of the U.S.-Japan trade deal finalized in July, Japan is expected to invest $550 billion to build and expand core U.S. industries, with US retaining 90% of the profits from these investments.

Japan is expected, as per the fact sheet released by the US government, to invest in energy infrastructure, semiconductor manufacturing, critical mineral mining, and commercial and defense ship building.

Under the deal terms, the Trump administration agreed to limit tariffs to 15% from the previous 25% on imported Japanese goods and Japan agreed to open up trade in U.S. goods, including cars, trucks and agricultural products.

data.title: Rubrik rises as enterprises seek more cyber resilience in age of AI: analysts
data.description:
Rubrik's (RBRK) third-quarter financial results demonstrated the data security firm is continuing to gain momentum for enterprises due in part to the rising risks of artificial intelligence threats, according to analysts.

Shares had surged 18% by early trading on Friday.

"Cloud ARR came in at $1.175 billion (up 53% y/y), well above the prior period of $1.064 billion as more customers shift into cloud and GenAI while choosing RBRK for comprehensive cyber resilience," said Wedbush analysts, led by Dan Ives, in a Friday investor note. "Total revenue came in at $350.2 million which was well above both the Street’s estimate of $320.5 million and the company’s guidance range of $319.0 million - $321.0 million while non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 came in significantly above both the Street's ($0.17) estimate and guidance of ($0.18) - ($0.16) as the company continues to see strong demand with cloud migrations accelerating due to AI-driven enterprise modernization."

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating and $120 price target on the stock.

"The company raised its FY26 guidance across the board, reflecting elevated demand as cyber resilience remains top of mind for CIOs and CISOs, with more organizations preparing for worst-case scenarios in the event of infrastructure breaches while AI continues to increase the threat level for enterprises," Ives added.

KeyBanc maintained its Overweight rating but slightly lowered its target price to $113 from $117 due to lower peer multiples. Meanwhile, William Blair upgraded the stock to Outperform from Market Perform due to its "stellar beat and raise."

Rubrik competitor Commvault Systems (CVLT) had inched down 0.2%.

"Our mission is to lead Rubrik into our next era as the, let me repeat, the security and AI operations company," said Rubrik CEO and co-founder Bipul Sinha, during the company's third-quarter earnings call. 

"Now with the rise of AI agents, a new transformation is underway," Sinha added. "AI transformation presents unique challenges. An agent operates within an organization's environment runs business processes, assumes identity, touches critical data and impacts mission-critical applications at unprecedented speed and scale. These agents are effectively superhuman, but organizations don't yet have the guardrails to monitor and contain what they do. We need to think about AI agents very differently. Through this AI transformation, Rubrik's mission and its strategy remains the same: to accelerate our customers' journey to AI enterprise."
